This flag can only be used along with [`EFFECT_FLAG_CANNOT_DISABLE`](/api/constants/EffectFlag/EFFECT_FLAG_CANNOT_DISABLE), since other effects can already become forbidden by default.

Name: EFFECT_FLAG_CARD_TARGET
Description: An [effect](/api/types/Effect) with this flag [targets](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Target) a card(s). Without this flag, an effect is not treated to be targetting even if [`Duel.SelectTarget`](/api/functions/Duel/SelectTarget).

Name: EFFECT_FLAG_CLIENT_HINT
Summary: Uses a string as a user hint.
Description: An [effect](/api/types/Effect) with this flag uses a string as a user hint, which is set using the effect's [`Effect.SetDescription`](/api/functions/Effect/SetDescription) call. If it affects a card(s), the string is displayed when hovering over the card. If it affects a player(s), the string is displayed when hovering over the player's LP bar.

Name: EFFECT_FLAG_CONTINUOUS_TARGET
Description: An [effect](/api/types/Effect) with this flag continuously targets a card. Effects that [equip](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Equip) have this flag inherently.

Name: EFFECT_FLAG_COPY_INHERIT
Summary: Will be copied when copying card effects even if it's not an initial effect.
Description: An [effect](/api/types/Effect) with this flag will be copied when copying a card's effects even if it's not an initial effect.

Name: EFFECT_FLAG_COUNT_LIMIT
Summary: Can only be used a limited number of times.
Description: An [effect](/api/types/Effect) with this flag can only be used a limited number of times. This flag is usually not through scripts directly, but is automatically added when calling [`Effect.SetCountLimit`](/api/functions/Effect/SetCountLimit).

Name: EFFECT_FLAG_DAMAGE_CAL
Summary: Can be activated during damage calculation.
Description: An [effect](/api/types/Effect) with this flag can be activated during [damage calculation](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Damage_calculation).

Name: EFFECT_FLAG_DAMAGE_STEP
Summary: Can be activated during the Damage Step.
Description: An [effect](/api/types/Effect) with this flag can be activated during the [Damage Step](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Damage_Step).

Name: EFFECT_FLAG_DELAY
Description: A trigger [effect](/api/types/Effect) with this flag can be activated even if its event requirement is not the last thing to happen, i.e, it is prevented from ["missing the timing"](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/If..._You_Can_VS_When..._You_Can).

Name: EFFECT_FLAG_EVENT_PLAYER
Description: An [effect](/api/types/Effect) with this flag is used by the player associated to the event that prompted it.

Name: EFFECT_FLAG_FIELD_ONLY
Summary: Does not belong to any card.
Description: An [effect](/api/types/Effect) with this flag does not belong to any card, but is applied globally in the Duel. This flag is usually not set through scripts directly, but is automatically added when registering an effect without a handler, i.e., using [`Duel.RegisterEffect`](/api/functions/Duel/RegisterEffect).

Name: EFFECT_FLAG_FUNC_VALUE
Summary: Has a function as an associated value.
Description: An [effect](/api/types/Effect) with this flag has a [function](/api/types/function) as an associated ["value"](/api/functions/Effect/SetValue). This flag is usually not set through scripts directly, but is automatically added when a function is passed as the second parameter to `Effect.SetValue`.

Name: EFFECT_FLAG_IGNORE_IMMUNE
Summary: Can affect cards that are unaffected by effects.
Description: An [effect](/api/types/Effect) with this flag can affect cards even if they are unaffected by effects.

Name: EFFECT_FLAG_IGNORE_RANGE
Summary: Affects cards of a player(s) in all locations.
Description: An [effect](/api/types/Effect) with this flag affects cards of a player(s) in all locations.

Name: EFFECT_FLAG_IMMEDIATELY_APPLY
Description: An [effect](/api/types/Effect) with this flag is applied immediately after being registered. Without this flag, effects are only enabled after certain actions are completed, such as Summoning, moving cards, and activating effects.

Name: EFFECT_FLAG_INITIAL
Summary: An original effect of a card.
Description: A card's original [effects](/api/types/Effect) have this flag. This flag is not set directly through scripts, but is automatically added to effects registered inside the `initial_effect` function.

Name: EFFECT_FLAG_LIMIT_ZONE
Summary: Limits the available zones on the field when activated.
Description: An [effect](/api/types/Effect) would limit the available zones on the field when activated. The remaining available zones must be specified in the effect's [value](/api/functions/Effect/SetValue).

Name: EFFECT_FLAG_NO_TURN_RESET
Summary: Count limit does not reset each turn.
Description: The use count limit of an [effect](/api/types/Effect) with this flag does not reset at the end of the turn. Usually used for texts such as `Once while face-up on the field, ...` (e.g., ["Baronne de Fleur"](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Baronne_de_Fleur)).

Name: EFFECT_FLAG_OATH
Summary: Removed if the activation of the effect that registered it is negated.
Description: An [effect](/api/types/Effect) that was registered with this flag during effect activation (e.g., in the [cost](/api/types/EffectCost) or [target](/api/types/EffectTarget) function) is removed if the activation of the effect that registered it is negated.

Name: EFFECT_FLAG_OWNER_RELATE
Summary: Does not apply if the card that registered it is no longer related.
Description: An [effect](/api/types/Effect) with this flag is not applied if the card that registered it is no longer "related" to the effect, such as if it has left the field or is negated.

Name: EFFECT_FLAG_PLAYER_TARGET
Description: An [effect](/api/types/Effect) with this flag affects a player(s).

Name: EFFECT_FLAG_SET_AVAILABLE
Description: An [effect](/api/types/Effect) with this flag also applies to face-down cards.

Name: EFFECT_FLAG_SINGLE_RANGE
Summary: Applies only to the card it's registered to while in a given location.
Description: An [effect](/api/types/Effect) with this flag only affects the card it's registered to while it's in the location defined in [`Effect.SetRange`](/api/functions/Effect/SetRange). It also doesn't apply if the card is on the field but is face-down.

Name: EFFECT_FLAG_SPSUM_PARAM
Summary: Special Summons in a given position and side of the field.
Description: A [Special Summon procedure](/api/constants/EffectCode/EFFECT_SPSUMMON_PROC) [effect](/api/types/Effect) with this flag will Special Summon the card in the [position](/api/enums/CardPosition) and side of the field specified as parameters of [`Effect.SetTargetRange`](/api/functions/Effect/SetTargetRange).

Name: EFFECT_FLAG_UNCOPYABLE
Summary: Cannot be copied by copying effects.
Description: An [effect](/api/types/Effect) with this flag cannot be copied by copying effects.

Name: EFFECT_FLAG2_CHECK_SIMULTANEOUS
Summary: Card activation that must be activated in a given location.
Description: A [card activation](/api/constants/EffectType/EFFECT_TYPE_ACTIVATE) [effect](/api/types/Effect) with this flag must be activated in the [location](/api/enums/Location) specified in [`Effect.SetRange`](/api/functions/Effect/SetRange).

Name: EFFECT_FLAG2_COF
Summary: Normal Spell activation that activates in the Standby Phase.
Description: A Normal Spell's [card activation](/api/constants/EffectType/EFFECT_TYPE_ACTIVATE) [effect](/api/types/Effect) with this flag activates during the Standby Phase. The name comes from ["Curse of Fiend"](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Curse_of_Fiend), the only card that had such effect.

Status.message: Constant is no longer used by "Curse of Fiend" or any other card.

Name: EFFECT_FLAG2_CONTINUOUS_EQUIP
Summary: Continuous Trap effects that can be activated while equipped.
Description: A Continuous Trap's trigger and fast [effects](/api/types/Effect) with this flag can be activated while the card is equipped to a monster.

Name: EFFECT_FLAG2_FORCE_ACTIVATE_LOCATION
Summary: Non-activated effect that can be copied by "Majestic Star Dragon".
Description: An [effect](/api/types/Effect) with this flag can be copied by ["Majestic Star Dragon"](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Majestic_Star_Dragon) even if it's not an activated effect.

Name: EFFECT_FLAG2_MAJESTIC_MUST_COPY
Summary: Non-activated effect that can be copied by "Majestic Star Dragon".
Description: An [effect](/api/types/Effect) with this flag can be copied by ["Majestic Star Dragon"](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Majestic_Star_Dragon) even if it's not an activated effect.

Name: EFFECT_MARKER_ALLURE_LVUP
Summary: Marks "Allure Queen" effects that activate by sending themselves to the GY.
Description: This marker must be used when [registering](/api/functions/Card/RegisterEffect) the effect of an ["Allure Queen"](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Allure_Queen) monster that activates by sending itself to the GY, so it could be identified by [the anime version of "Allure Palace"](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Allure_Palace_(anime)).

Name: EFFECT_MARKER_CARDIAN
Description: This marker must be used when [registering](/api/functions/Card/RegisterEffect) the Summoning condition of a ["Flower Cardian"](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Allure_Queen) monster, so it could be identified by various "Flower Cardian" anime cards.

Name: EFFECT_MARKER_DETACH_XMAT
Summary: Marks Xyz Monster effects that detach materials from themselves as cost.
Description: This marker must be used when [registering](/api/functions/Card/RegisterEffect) the effect of an Xyz Monster that detaches Xyz materials from itself as cost, so it could be identified by ["Tachyon Unit"](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Tachyon_Unit).

Name: EFFECT_MARKER_DRAGON_RULER
Summary: Marks "Dragon Ruler" effects that activate by discarding themselves.
Description: This marker must be used when [registering](/api/functions/Card/RegisterEffect) the effect of a ["Dragon Ruler"](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Dragon_Ruler) monster that activates by discarding itself, so it could be identified by ["Chasmatis, Dragon Ruler of Auroras"](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Chasmatis,_Dragon_Ruler_of_Auroras).

Name: EFFECT_MARKER_TELLAR
Summary: Marks "tellarknight"/"Constellar" effects that activate when Normal Summoned.
Description: This marker must be used when [registering](/api/functions/Card/RegisterEffect) the effect of a ["tellarknight"](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/tellarknight) or ["Constellar"](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Constellar) monster that activates when the card is Normal Summoned, so it could be identified by ["Tellarknight Constellar Caduceus"](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Tellarknight_Constellar_Caduceus).

Name: EFFECT_MARKER_THUNDRA
Summary: Marks "Thunder Dragon" effects that activate by discarding themselves.
Description: This marker must be used when [registering](/api/functions/Card/RegisterEffect) the effect of a ["Thunder Dragon"](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Thunder_Dragon) monster that activates by discarding itself, so it could be identified by ["Thunder Dragon Thunderstormech"](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Thunder_Dragon_Thunderstormech).

Name: EFFECT_TYPE_ACTIONS
Description: The type for [activated](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Activate) [effects](/api/types/Effect), i.e., effects that form a Chain Link. This type is not set directly through scripts, but is added automatically depending on the effect's other types.

Name: EFFECT_TYPE_ACTIVATE
Description: The type for [effects](/api/types/Effect) that define Spell/Trap Card activation, i.e., playing a Spell/Trap Card on the field face-up from the hand or flipping it from face-down on the field.

Name: EFFECT_TYPE_CONTINUOUS
Description: The type for [effects](/api/types/Effect) that are continuously applied.

Name: EFFECT_TYPE_EQUIP
Summary: Applied by an Equip Card to the equipped monster
Description: The type for [effects](/api/types/Effect) that are applied by an Equip Card to the monster they are equipped to.

Name: EFFECT_TYPE_FIELD
Summary: Applies to players or multiple cards, or listens for events anywhere in the Duel
Description: The type for [effects](/api/types/Effect) that can apply to players or multiple cards in the Duel. If combined with [`EFFECT_TYPE_CONTINUOUS`](/api/constants/EffectType/EFFECT_TYPE_CONTINUOUS), [`EFFECT_TYPE_TRIGGER_F`](/api/constants/EffectType/EFFECT_TYPE_TRIGGER_F), or [`EFFECT_TYPE_TRIGGER_O`](/api/constants/EffectType/EFFECT_TYPE_TRIGGER_O), the effect will be prompted or triggered when the event specified in [`Effect.SetCode`](/api/functions/Effect/SetCode) happens anywhere in the Duel.

Name: EFFECT_TYPE_FLIP
Description: The type for FLIP effects. An effect with this type will also automatically have [`EFFECT_TYPE_TRIGGER_F`](/api/constants/EffectType/EFFECT_TYPE_TRIGGER_F) as its type, and [`EVENT_FLIP`](/api/constants/Event/EVENT_FLIP) as its [effect code](/api/functions/Effect/SetCode).

Name: EFFECT_TYPE_GRANT
Summary: Grants another effect to other cards
Description: The type for [effects](/api/types/Effect) that grant another effect to other cards, e.g., ["The Weather"](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/The_Weather) Continous Spells and Traps. The effect to be granted must be set as the [label object](/api/functions/Effect/SetLabelObject) of the effect with this type. This excludes effects that are granted by Xyz materials to the Xyz monster they are attached to, which should have [`EFFECT_TYPE_XMATERIAL`](/api/constants/EffectType/EFFECT_TYPE_XMATERIAL) instead.

Name: EFFECT_TYPE_IGNITION
Description: The type for [Ignition Effects](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Ignition_Effect), i.e., [Spell Speed 1](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Spell_Speed_1) effects that the turn player can choose to activate during their Main Phase in an open game state.

Name: EFFECT_TYPE_QUICK_F
Description: The type for **mandatory** [fast](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Fast_effect) [effects](/api/types/Effect), i.e., [Spell Speed 2](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Spell_Speed_2) effects that will always activate when their condition is met.

Name: EFFECT_TYPE_QUICK_O
Description: The type for **optional** [fast](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Fast_effect) [effects](/api/types/Effect), i.e., [Spell Speed 2](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Spell_Speed_2) effects that the player can choose to activate when their condition is met. The exceptions to this are Spell/Trap Card activation effects, which should have [`EFFECT_TYPE_ACTIVATE`](/api/constants/EffectType/EFFECT_TYPE_ACTIVATE) instead even if they are Spell Speed 2 (e.g., Normal Traps and Quick-Play Spells).

Name: EFFECT_TYPE_SINGLE
Summary: Applies only to the handler, or listens for events happening to the handler
Description: The type for [effects](/api/types/Effect) that only apply to the card it belongs to. If combined with [`EFFECT_TYPE_CONTINUOUS`](/api/constants/EffectType/EFFECT_TYPE_CONTINUOUS), [`EFFECT_TYPE_TRIGGER_F`](/api/constants/EffectType/EFFECT_TYPE_TRIGGER_F), or [`EFFECT_TYPE_TRIGGER_O`](/api/constants/EffectType/EFFECT_TYPE_TRIGGER_O), the effect will only be prompted or triggered when the event specified in [`Effect.SetCode`](/api/functions/Effect/SetCode) happens to the card itself.

Name: EFFECT_TYPE_TARGET
Description: The type for Spell/Trap [effects](/api/types/Effect) that continuously target a card.

Status.message: Constant is unused, and there are functions that handle continuous targetting.

Name: EFFECT_TYPE_TRIGGER_F
Description: The type for **mandatory** [Trigger](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Trigger_Effect) [Effects](/api/types/Effect), i.e., [Spell Speed 1](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Spell_Speed_1) effects that will always activate *in a new Chain* after their condition is met.

Name: EFFECT_TYPE_TRIGGER_O
Description: The type for **optional** [Trigger](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Trigger_Effect) [Effects](/api/types/Effect), i.e., [Spell Speed 1](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Spell_Speed_1) effects that the player can choose to activate *in a new Chain* after their condition is met.

Name: EFFECT_TYPE_XMATERIAL
Summary: Granted by Xyz materials to the Xyz monster they are attached to
Description: The type for [effects](/api/types/Effect) that are granted by Xyz materials to the Xyz monster they are attached to.

Name: EVENT_ADD_COUNTER
Summary: A counter(s) is placed on a card(s).
Description: This event is raised when a counter(s) is placed on a card(s).

Name: EVENT_ADJUST
Description: This event is raised when the game state changes.

Name: EVENT_ATTACK_ANNOUNCE
Description: This event is raised when an attack is declared.

Name: EVENT_ATTACK_DISABLED
Description: This event is raised when an attack is negated.

Name: EVENT_BATTLED
Description: This event is raised after [damage calculation](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Damage_calculation).

Name: EVENT_BATTLE_CONFIRM
Summary: A monster is battling another monster.
Description: This event is raised when a monster battles another monster.

Name: EVENT_BATTLE_DAMAGE
Description: This event is raised when a player takes battle damage.

Name: EVENT_BATTLE_DESTROYED
Summary: A monster is destroyed by battle.
Description: This event is raised after a monster is destroyed by battle. This event is considered to happen to the monster that is destroyed, unlike [`EVENT_BATTLE_DESTROYING`](/api/constants/Event/EVENT_BATTLE_DESTROYING).

Name: EVENT_BATTLE_DESTROYING
Summary: A monster destroys another monster by battle.
Description: This event is raised when a monster destroys another monster by battle. This event is considered to happen to the monster that destroys the other monster, unlike [`EVENT_BATTLE_DESTROYED`](/api/constants/Event/EVENT_BATTLE_DESTROYED).

Name: EVENT_BATTLE_START
Description: This event is raised at the start of the [Damage Step](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Damage_Step).

Name: EVENT_BECOME_TARGET
Summary: A card(s) is targeted by an effect.
Description: This event is raised when a card(s) is [targeted](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Target) by an effect.

Name: EVENT_BE_BATTLE_TARGET
Summary: A monster is targeted for an attack.
Description: This event is raised when a monster is targeted for an attack.

Name: EVENT_BE_MATERIAL
Summary: A card(s) is used as a material for a Summon.
Description: This event is raised when a card(s) is used as a material for a Summon. Since the event is raised **after** a Summon has been conducted, some properties of the material might have changed. If an effect needs to check or store some information that might change after the Summon, it should use [`EVENT_BE_PRE_MATERIAL`](/api/constants/Event/EVENT_BE_PRE_MATERIAL) instead.

Name: EVENT_BE_PRE_MATERIAL
Summary: A card(s) is about to be used as a material for a Summon.
Description: This event is raised when a card(s) is about to be used as a material for a Summon. Since the event is raised **before** a Summon is conducted, it is typically used to check or store some information that might change after the Summon, since [`EVENT_BE_MATERIAL`](/api/constants/Event/EVENT_BE_MATERIAL) would be raised too late.

Name: EVENT_BREAK_EFFECT
Description: This event is raised when the [`Duel.BreakEffect`](/api/functions/Duel/BreakEffect) function is executed.

Name: EVENT_CHAINING
Summary: An effect is activated, creating a Chain Link.
Description: This event is raised when an effect is activated, i.e., a Chain Link is created.

Name: EVENT_CHAIN_ACTIVATING
Summary: A Chain Link is about to resolve.
Description: This event is raised when a Chain Link is about to resolve before applying any other effect (such as effects that apply on [`EVENT_CHAIN_SOLVING`](/api/constants/Event/EVENT_CHAIN_SOLVING)). Typically used for mandatory continuous negation effects (e.g., ["Shadow-Imprisoning Mirror"](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Silent_Graveyard)) to make sure they are applied first and not allow other effects to respond to a negated effect.

Name: EVENT_CHAIN_DISABLED
Summary: The effect of a Chain Link is negated.
Description: This event is raised when the effect of Chain Link is negated. This does not include when the **activation** of an effect is negated ([`EVENT_CHAIN_NEGATED`](/api/constants/Event/EVENT_CHAIN_NEGATED) is raised instead).

Name: EVENT_CHAIN_END
Description: This event is raised when an entire Chain has fully resolved.

Name: EVENT_CHAIN_NEGATED
Summary: The activation of an effect is negated.
Description: This event is raised when the activation of an effect is negated. This does not include when an effect of a Chain Link is negated ([`EVENT_CHAIN_DISABLED`](/api/constants/Event/EVENT_CHAIN_DISABLED) is raised instead).

Name: EVENT_CHAIN_SOLVED
Summary: A Chain Link has finished resolving.
Description: This event is raised when a Chain Link has finished resolving.

Name: EVENT_CHAIN_SOLVING
Description: This event is raised when resolving a Chain Link that has not been negated (such as by an effect applied on [`EVENT_CHAIN_ACTIVATING`](/api/constants/Event/EVENT_CHAIN_ACTIVATING)). Typically used for effects that can optionally respond to a resolving effect.

Name: EVENT_CHANGE_POS
Summary: A monster's battle position is changed.
Description: This event is raised when a monster's [battle position](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Battle_position) is changed.

Name: EVENT_CONTROL_CHANGED
Summary: The control of a card is changed.
Description: This event is raised when the control of a card changes.

Name: EVENT_CUSTOM
Summary: Added to a custom ID to create a custom event.
Description: This is added to a custom ID (e.g., `EVENT_CUSTOM+id`) to create a custom event which can be manually raised using [`Duel.RaiseEvent`](/api/functions/Duel/RaiseEvent) or [`Duel.RaiseSingleEvent`](/api/functions/Duel/RaiseSingleEvent).

Name: EVENT_DAMAGE
Summary: A player took battle or effect damage.
Description: This event is raised when a player takes battle or effect damage.

Name: EVENT_DAMAGE_STEP_END
Description: This event is raised at the [end of the Damage Step](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Damage_Step#End_of_the_Damage_Step).

Name: EVENT_DESTROY
Summary: A card(s) is destroyed but has not yet left the field.
Description: This event is raised when a card(s) is [destroyed](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Destroy) but has not yet left the field. This allows accessing information about a card that might change once it leaves the field and [`EVENT_DESTROYED`](/api/constants/Event/EVENT_DESTROYED) is raised, such as its type, attribute, counters placed on it, and effects applied to it.

Name: EVENT_DESTROYED
Summary: A card(s) is destroyed and has left the field.
Description: This event is raised when a card(s) is [destroyed](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Destroy). Since the card has already left the field, some of its information might have changed from when it was on the field, such as its type, attribute, counters placed on it, and effects applied to it. If those are needed, [`EVENT_DESTROY`](/api/constants/Event/EVENT_DESTROY) should be used instead.

Name: EVENT_DETACH_MATERIAL
Summary: An Xyz Monster's material(s) is detached.
Description: This event is raised when an [Xyz Monster](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Xyz_Monster)'s material(s) are [detached](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Detach). This event is considered to happen to the Xyz Monster, not the detached cards.

Name: EVENT_DISCARD
Description: This event is raised when a card(s) is [discarded](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Discard).

Name: EVENT_DRAW
Description: This event is raised when a card(s) is [drawn](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Draw_a_card).

Name: EVENT_EQUIP
Description: This event is raised when a card(s) is [equipped](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Equip).

Name: EVENT_FLIP
Description: This event is raised when a card is flipped face-up. It does not cover flipping face-down, for which [`EVENT_CHANGE_POS`](/api/constants/Event/EVENT_CHANGE_POS) should be used instead.

Name: EVENT_FLIP_SUMMON
Summary: A monster would be Flip Summoned.
Description: This event is raised when a monster would be [Flip Summoned](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Flip_Summon). This is when cards like ["Solemn Judgment"](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Solemn_Judgment) can respond, as opposed to [`EVENT_FLIP_SUMMON_SUCCESS`](/api/constants/Event/EVENT_FLIP_SUMMON_SUCCESS), which is raised when the Flip Summon is completed.

Name: EVENT_FLIP_SUMMON_NEGATED
Summary: The Flip Summon of a monster is negated.
Description: This event is raised when the [Flip Summon](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Flip_Summon) of a monster is negated.

Name: EVENT_FLIP_SUMMON_SUCCESS
Summary: A monster is Flip Summoned successfully.
Description: This event is raised when a monster is Flip Summoned successfully. This is when cards like ["Bottomless Trap Hole"](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Bottomless_Trap_Hole) can respond, as opposed to [`EVENT_FLIP_SUMMON`](/api/constants/Event/EVENT_FLIP_SUMMON), which is raised when a monster is about to be Flip Summoned.

Name: EVENT_FREE_CHAIN
Summary: A card or effect can be activated freely.
Description: Represents the activation window for a card or effect that can be activated at any time (provided they meet their condition). This event is not raised by any specific action but is used to indicate that a card or effect can be activated freely.

Name: EVENT_LEAVE_FIELD
Description: This event is raised when a card(s) leaves the field. Since the card has already left the field, some of its information might have changed from when it was on the field, such as its type, attribute, counters placed on it, and effects applied to it. If those are needed, [`EVENT_LEAVE_FIELD_P`](/api/constants/Event/EVENT_LEAVE_FIELD_P) should be used instead.

Name: EVENT_LEAVE_FIELD_P
Summary: A card(s) is about to leave the field.
Description: This event is raised when a card(s) is about to leave the field. This allows accessing information about a card that might change once it leaves the field and [`EVENT_LEAVE_FIELD`](/api/constants/Event/EVENT_LEAVE_FIELD) is raised, such as its type, attribute, counters placed on it, and effects applied to it.

Name: EVENT_LEVEL_UP
Description: This event is raised when the Level of a monster is increased.

Name: EVENT_MOVE
Summary: A card(s) is moved to a different location or sequence.
Description: This event is raised when a card(s) is moved to a different [location](/api/enums/Location) or sequence.

Name: EVENT_MSET
Description: This event is raised when a monster is [Normal Set](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Normal_Set).

Name: EVENT_PAY_LPCOST
Summary: A player paid LP as a cost.
Description: This event is raised when a player pays LP as a cost.

Name: EVENT_PHASE
Description: This event is raised when a given [phase](/api/enums/Phase) is reached, specified by combining it with the corresponding phase value (e.g., `EVENT_PHASE|PHASE_DRAW` for the Draw Phase).

Name: EVENT_PHASE_START
Summary: The start of a given phase.
Description: This event is raised at the start of a given [phase](/api/enums/Phase), specified by combining it with the corresponding phase value (e.g., `EVENT_PHASE_START|PHASE_DRAW` for the start of the Draw Phase).

Name: EVENT_PREDRAW
Summary: Start of the Draw Phase, before the turn player draws a card.
Description: This event is raised at the start of the [Draw Phase](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Draw_Phase), before the turn player draws a card for their [normal draw](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Normal_draw).

Name: EVENT_PRE_BATTLE_DAMAGE
Summary: A player is about to take battle damage.
Description: This event is raised when a player is about to take battle damage.

Name: EVENT_PRE_DAMAGE_CALCULATE
Summary: Damage calculation is about to be performed.
Description: This event is raised when [damage calculation](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Damage_calculation) is about to be performed.

Name: EVENT_RECOVER
Description: This event is raised when a player gains LP.

Name: EVENT_RELEASE
Description: This event is raised when a card(s) is Tributed.

Name: EVENT_REMOVE
Description: This event is raised when a card(s) is banished.

Name: EVENT_REMOVE_COUNTER
Summary: A counter is removed from cards.
Description: This event is raised when a counter is removed from cards.

Name: EVENT_RETURN_TO_GRAVE
Summary: A card(s) is being returned to the GY.
Description: This event was raised when a card(s) is being returned to the GY.

Status.message: Event is no longer raised.

Name: EVENT_SPSUMMON
Summary: A monster would be Special Summoned.
Description: This event is raised when a monster would be [Special Summoned](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Special_Summon). This is when cards like ["Solemn Judgment"](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Solemn_Judgment) can respond, as opposed to [`EVENT_SPSUMMON_SUCCESS`](/api/constants/Event/EVENT_SPSUMMON_SUCCESS), which is raised when the Special Summon is completed.

Name: EVENT_SPSUMMON_NEGATED
Summary: The Special Summon of a monster is negated.
Description: This event is raised when the [Special Summon](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Special_Summon) of a monster is negated.

Name: EVENT_SPSUMMON_SUCCESS
Summary: A monster is Special Summoned successfully.
Description: This event is raised when a monster is Special Summoned successfully. This is when cards like ["Bottomless Trap Hole"](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Bottomless_Trap_Hole) can respond, as opposed to [`EVENT_SPSUMMON`](/api/constants/Event/EVENT_SPSUMMON), which is raised when a monster is about to be Special Summoned.

Name: EVENT_SSET
Description: This event is raised when a Spell/Trap Card is [Set](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Set).

Name: EVENT_STARTUP
Description: This event is raised at the start of the Duel.

Name: EVENT_SUMMON
Summary: A monster would be Normal Summoned.
Description: This event is raised when a monster would be [Normal Summoned](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Normal_Summon). This is when cards like ["Solemn Judgment"](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Solemn_Judgment) can respond, as opposed to [`EVENT_SUMMON_SUCCESS`](/api/constants/Event/EVENT_SUMMON_SUCCESS), which is raised when the Normal Summon is completed.

Name: EVENT_SUMMON_NEGATED
Summary: The Normal Summon of a monster is negated.
Description: This event is raised when the [Normal Summon](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Normal_Summon) of a monster is negated.

Name: EVENT_SUMMON_SUCCESS
Summary: A monster is Normal Summoned successfully.
Description: This event is raised when a monster is Normal Summoned successfully. This is when cards like ["Bottomless Trap Hole"](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Bottomless_Trap_Hole) can respond, as opposed to [`EVENT_SUMMON`](/api/constants/Event/EVENT_SUMMON), which is raised when a monster is about to be Normal Summoned.

Name: EVENT_TOSS_COIN
Description: This event is raised when a coin is tossed. At this point, the result is final and the toss can no longer be redone, unlike [`EVENT_TOSS_COIN_NEGATE`](/api/constants/Event/EVENT_TOSS_COIN_NEGATE).

Name: EVENT_TOSS_COIN_NEGATE
Summary: A coin toss has just been performed and can be redone.
Description: This event is raised when a coin toss has just been performed and can be redone, resulting in a different result.

Name: EVENT_TOSS_DICE
Description: This event is raised when a six-sided die is rolled. At this point, the result is final and the roll can no longer be redone, unlike [`EVENT_TOSS_DICE_NEGATE`](/api/constants/Event/EVENT_TOSS_DICE_NEGATE).

Name: EVENT_TOSS_DICE_NEGATE
Summary: A six-sided die has just been rolled and can be redone.
Description: This event is raised when a six-sided die has just been rolled and can be redone, resulting in a different result.

Name: EVENT_TO_DECK
Summary: A card(s) is sent to the Deck.
Description: This event is raised when a card(s) is sent to either Main or Extra Deck.

Name: EVENT_TO_GRAVE
Summary: A card(s) is sent to the GY.
Description: This event is raised when a card(s) is sent to the GY.

Name: EVENT_TO_HAND
Summary: A card(s) is added to the hand.
Description: This event is raised when a card(s) is added to the hand.

Name: EVENT_TURN_END
Description: This event is raised at the end of the turn (after the End Phase).

Name: FUSPROC_CANCELABLE
Summary: Can be canceled when selecting the Fusion materials.
Description: A Fusion Summoning [effect](/api/types/Effect] created with this flag can be canceled when selecting the Fusion materials. This flag is set by default, meaning most Fusion Summons can be canceled. The only exceptions are Summoning effects with preset materials (e.g., ["Clock Lizard"](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Clock_Lizard)).

Name: FUSPROC_CONTACTFUS
Description: A Fusion Summoning [effect](/api/types/Effect] created with this flag performs a [Contact Fusion](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Contact_Fusion). This flag is usually not set directly in card scripts, but is set automatically by [`Fusion.AddContactProc`](/api/functions/Fusion/AddContactProc).

Name: FUSPROC_LISTEDMATS
Summary: Uses the materials listed on the Fusion Monster.
Description: This flag is used in addition to [`FUSPROC_NOTFUSION`](/api/constants/FusionProcFlag/FUSPROC_NOTFUSION) if the Summoning effect (that is not treated as a Fusion Summon) uses the materials listed on the Fusion Monster. This is not relevant for actual Fusion Summons, since they already use the listed materials by default.

Name: FUSPROC_NOLIMIT
Description: A Fusion Summoning [effect](/api/types/Effect] created with this flag ignores [Summoning conditions](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Summoning_condition), such as `Must be Special Summoned with "..."`.

Name: FUSPROC_NOTFUSION
Summary: Not treated as a Fusion Summon.
Description: This flag is used for effects that use the Fusion Summon procedure but do not count as a Fusion Summon, such as ["Favorite Contact"](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Favorite_Contact).

Name: GLOBALFLAG_BRAINWASHING_CHECK
Summary: Enables effects that return the control of monsters to their owner.
Description: This global flag enables [effects](/api/types/Effect) with [`EFFECT_REMOVE_BRAINWASHING`](/api/constants/EffectCode/EFFECT_REMOVE_BRAINWASHING) as [code](/api/enums/EffectCode), which continuously return the control of affected monsters to their owner, i.e., the effect of ["Remove Brainwashing"](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Remove_Brainwashing). It is automatically set when an effect with `EFFECT_REMOVE_BRAINWASHING` as code is registered.

Name: GLOBALFLAG_DECK_REVERSE_CHECK
Summary: Allows face-up cards in the Main Deck.
Description: This global flag allows face-up cards in the Main Deck, for [effects](/api/types/Effect) that add cards to the Main Deck face-up or flip cards in the Main Deck face-up. It is automatically set when an effect with [`EFFECT_REVERSE_DECK`](/api/constants/EffectCode/EFFECT_REVERSE_DECK) as [code](/api/enums/EffectCode) is registered, or when [`Duel.ReverseInDeck`](/api/functions/Card/ReverseInDeck) is called.

Name: GLOBALFLAG_DELAYED_QUICKEFFECT
Description: This global flag is no longer used and has been deleted.

Name: GLOBALFLAG_DETACH_EVENT
Summary: Allows effects to respond when an Xyz Monster's material(s) are detached.
Description: This global flag enables the [`EVENT_DETACH_MATERIAL`](/api/constants/Event/EVENT_DETACH_MATERIAL) event to be raised when an Xyz Monster's material(s) are detached. It is automatically set when an effect with `EVENT_DETACH_MATERIAL` as [event code](/api/enums/Event) is registered.

Name: GLOBALFLAG_SELF_TOGRAVE
Summary: Enables effects that continuously send their handler to the GY.
Description: This global flag enables [effects](/api/types/Effect) with [`EFFECT_SELF_TOGRAVE`](/api/constants/EffectCode/EFFECT_SELF_TOGRAVE) as [code](/api/enums/EffectCode), which continuously send their handler to the GY when their condition is met. It is automatically set when an effect with `EFFECT_SELF_TOGRAVE` as code is registered.

Name: GLOBALFLAG_SPSUMMON_COUNT
Summary: Enabled effects that limit the number of times players can Special Summon.
Description: This global flag was used to enable [effects](/api/types/Effect) with [`EFFECT_SPSUMMON_COUNT_LIMIT`](/api/constants/EffectCode/EFFECT_SPSUMMON_COUNT_LIMIT) as [code](/api/enums/EffectCode), which limit the number of times affected players can attempt to Special Summon per turn.

Status.message: `EFFECT_SPSUMMON_COUNT_LIMIT` effects no longer need this flag.

Name: GLOBALFLAG_SPSUMMON_ONCE
Summary: Enables restricting a monster to being Special Summoned only once per turn.
Description: This global flag enables restricting a monster to being Special Summoned only once per turn, e.g., conditions such as `You can only Special Summon "..." once per turn`. It is automatically set when [`Card.SetSPSummonOnce`](/api/functions/Card/SetSPSummonOnce) is called.

Name: GLOBALFLAG_XMAT_COUNT_LIMIT
Summary: Allowed EFFECT_TYPE_XMATERIAL effects to have count limits.
Description: This global flag allowed [effects](/api/types/Effect) with [`EFFECT_TYPE_XMATERIAL`](/api/constants/EffectType/EFFECT_TYPE_XMATERIAL) as [code](/api/enums/EffectCode) to have [count limits](/api/functions/Effect/SetCountLimit).

Status.message: `EFFECT_TYPE_XMATERIAL` effects no longer need this flag to have count limits.

Name: HINTMSG_APPLYTO
Summary: "Select the card(s) to apply the effect to"

Name: HINT_CARD
Description: Describes a hint that shows a card specified by its passcode as the hint value.

Name: HINT_MESSAGE
Description: Describes a hint that shows a message specified by its string ID as the hint value.

Name: HINT_NUMBER
Summary: Shows a number or prompts a player to declare a number.
Description: Describes a hint that shows a number specified by the hint value. If this constant is used as a hint value instead, it prompts a player to declare a number.

Name: HINT_OPSELECTED
Summary: Shows the option selected by the opponent.
Description: Describes a hint that shows a message, specified by its string ID as the hint value, indicating what option the opponent selected.

Name: HINT_SELECTMSG
Summary: Prompts a player to select a card(s).
Description: Describes a hint that prompts a player to select a card(s). The prompt string is a [hint message](/api/enums/HintMessage) specified as the hint value.

Name: HINT_ZONE
Description: Describes a hint that shows a zone(s) specified as the hint value.

Name: LOCATION_ALL
Description: The total value of all locations.

Name: LOCATION_DECK
Description: The [Main Deck](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Main_Deck). When used as a redirect location, the card(s) is placed on top of the Deck.

Name: LOCATION_EMZONE
Description: The [Extra Monster Zone](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Extra_Monster_Zone).

Name: LOCATION_EXTRA
Description: The [Extra Deck](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Extra_Deck).

Name: LOCATION_FZONE
Description: The [Field Zone](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Field_Zone).

Name: LOCATION_GRAVE
Description: The [GY](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Graveyard).

Name: LOCATION_HAND
Description: The [hand](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Hand).

Name: LOCATION_MMZONE
Description: The [Main Monster Zone](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Main_Monster_Zone).

Name: LOCATION_MZONE
Description: The [Monster Zone](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Monster_Zone).

Name: LOCATION_ONFIELD
Description: All zones on the field, consisting of the [Monster Zone](/api/constants/Location/LOCATION_MZONE) and the [location for Spells and Traps](/api/constants/Location/LOCATION_SZONE).

Name: LOCATION_OVERLAY
Description: The location for cards attached as Xyz Material. This is also used by some anime cards as the location for cards that have been "stacked" over.

Name: LOCATION_PUBLIC
Description: The [field](/api/constants/Location/LOCATION_ONFIELD), [banishment](/api/constants/Location/LOCATION_REMOVED), and [GY](/api/constants/Location/LOCATION_GRAVE), i.e., locations where cards are usually public knowledge.

Name: LOCATION_PZONE
Description: The [Pendulum Zone](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Pendulum_Zone).

Name: LOCATION_REMOVED
Description: The location for [banished](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Banish) cards.

Name: LOCATION_STZONE
Description: The [Spell & Trap Zones](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Spell_%26_Trap_Zone). This excludes the [Field Zone](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Field_Zone) and the separate [Pendulum Zones](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Pendulum_Zone) in Master Rule 3.

Name: LOCATION_SZONE
Description: The location for Spells and Traps, consisting of the [Spell & Trap Zones](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Spell_%26_Trap_Zone), the [Field Zone](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Field_Zone), and the [Pendulum Zones](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Pendulum_Zone).

Name: LOCATION_REASON_CONTROL
Summary: If a zone is available for the purpose of changing the control of a monster.
Description: This is used as the reason when checking if a zone is available for the purpose of changing the control of a monster. For example, to take control of an opponent's monster, a player must have at least one Main Monster Zone available.

Name: LOCATION_REASON_COUNT
Summary: If there is a zone that can be disabled.
Description: This is used as the reason when checking if there is a zone that can be disabled (become unusable).

Name: LOCATION_REASON_TOFIELD
Summary: If a zone is available to place a card in.
Description: This is used as the reason when checking if a zone is available to place a card in. This is the default reason for [`Duel.GetLocationCount`](/api/functions/Duel/GetLocationCount) and is typically not set in card scripts.

Name: PHASE_BATTLE
Description: The end of the [Battle Phase](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Battle_Phase).

Name: PHASE_BATTLE_START
Summary: Start Step of the Battle Phase.
Description: The [Start Step](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Start_Step), the first step of the [Battle Phase](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Battle_Phase).

Name: PHASE_BATTLE_STEP
Summary: Battle Step of the Battle Phase.
Description: The [Battle Step](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Battle_Step), one of the four steps of the [Battle Phase](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Battle_Phase).

Name: PHASE_DAMAGE_CAL

Name: PHASE_DRAW
Description: The [Draw Phase](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Draw_Phase).

Name: PHASE_END
Description: The [End Phase](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/End_Phase).

Name: PHASE_MAIN1
Description: The [Main Phase 1](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Main_Phase_1).

Name: PHASE_MAIN2
Description: The [Main Phase 2](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Main_Phase_2).

Name: PHASE_STANDBY
Description: The [Standby Phase](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Standby_Phase).

Name: PLAYER_ALL
Description: Refers to both players.

Name: PLAYER_EITHER
Description: Refers to either player.

Name: PLAYER_NONE
Description: Refers to neither player.

Name: REASON_ADJUST
Summary: Caused by adjusting a player's hand size at the end of the turn.
Description: The reason value for actions caused by adjusting a player's hand size at the end of the turn.

Name: REASON_BATTLE
Summary: Caused by destroying a card(s) by battle.
Description: The reason value for actions caused by destroying a card(s) by battle.

Name: REASON_COST
Description: The reason value used for actions performed as costs. This also includes actions caused by failure to pay maintenance costs.

Name: REASON_DESTROY
Description: The reason value for actions caused by destroying a card(s).

Name: REASON_DISCARD
Description: The reason value for actions caused by discarding a card(s).

Name: REASON_DISSUMMON
Description: The reason value for actions caused by negating a Summon.

Name: REASON_DRAW
Description: The reason value for actions caused by drawing a card(s).

Name: REASON_EFFECT
Summary: Performed during the resolution of an effect.
Description: The reason value used for actions performed during the resolution of an effect.

Name: REASON_FLIP
Description: The reason value is currently unused.

Status.message: Value is currently unused.

Name: REASON_FUSION
Summary: Caused by using a card as Fusion Material.
Description: The reason value for actions caused by using a card as Fusion Material.

Name: REASON_LINK
Summary: Caused by using a card as Link Material.
Description: The reason value for actions caused by using a card as Link Material.

Name: REASON_LOST_TARGET
Summary: Caused by an losing the equip target of an Equip Card.
Description: The reason value for actions caused by losing the equip target of an Equip Card, e.g., it leaves the field or is flipped face-down.

Name: REASON_MATERIAL
Summary: Caused by using a card as material for a Summon.
Description: The reason value for actions caused by using a card as material for a Summon.

Name: REASON_REDIRECT
Summary: Caused by redirecting a card's destination.
Description: The reason value for actions caused by redirecting a card's destination, e.g., `Banish it when it leaves the field`.

Name: REASON_RELEASE
Description: The reason value for actions caused by Tributing a card(s).

Name: REASON_REPLACE
Description: The reason value for actions caused by replacing card destruction, e.g., `If this card would be destroyed, you can banish 1 monster from your GY instead`.

Name: REASON_RETURN
Summary: Caused by returning a banished card to the GY.
Description: The reason value for actions caused by returning a banished card to the GY.

Name: REASON_EXCAVATE
Description: The reason value for actions performed after excavating a card.

Name: REASON_RITUAL
Summary: Caused by using a card as Ritual Material.
Description: The reason value for actions caused by using a card as Ritual Material.

Name: REASON_RULE
Description: The reason value used for actions caused by game mechanics, i.e., not part of any player's actions or any card effects.

Name: REASON_SPSUMMON
Summary: Caused by Special Summoning a monster.
Description: The reason value for actions caused by Special Summoning a monster.

Name: REASON_SUMMON
Summary: Caused by Normal Summoning a monster.
Description: The reason value for actions caused by Normal Summoning a monster.

Name: REASON_SYNCHRO
Summary: Caused by Synchro Summoning a monster.
Description: The reason value for actions caused by Synchro Summoning a monster.

Name: REASON_TEMPORARY
Description: The reason value used when banishing a monster temporarily.

Name: REASON_XYZ
Summary: Caused by using a card as Xyz Material.
Description: The reason value for actions caused by using a card as Xyz Material.

Name: LOCATION_DECKBOT
Summary: Location for cards sent to the bottom of the Deck upon redirection.
Description: Location for cards sent to the bottom of the Deck upon redirection.

Name: LOCATION_DECKSHF
Summary: Location for cards shuffled into the Deck upon redirection.
Description: Location for cards shuffled into the Deck upon redirection.

Name: RESETS_CANNOT_ACT
Summary: Typically used for effects that prevent a card's effects from being activated.
Description: A composite [reset value](/api/functions/Effect/SetReset) typically used for effects that prevent a card's effects from being activated. An [effect](/api/types/Effect) with this reset value added to [`RESET_EVENT`](/api/constants/Reset/RESET_EVENT) resets when [the card it's registered to](/api/functions/Effect/GetHandler) [stops being face-up in its current location](/api/constants/Reset/RESETS_STANDARD), **except** when it [leaves the field](/api/constants/Reset/RESET_LEAVE).

Name: RESETS_REDIRECT
Summary: Typically used for effects that redirect a card when it leaves the field.
Description: A composite [reset value](/api/functions/Effect/SetReset) typically used for effects that redirect a card to a different location when it leaves the field. An [effect](/api/types/Effect) with this reset value added to [`RESET_EVENT`](/api/constants/Reset/RESET_EVENT) resets when [the card it's registered to](/api/functions/Effect/GetHandler) [stops being face-up in its current location](/api/constants/Reset/RESETS_STANDARD), **except** when it [leaves the field](/api/constants/Reset/RESET_LEAVE) or is [sent to the GY](/api/constants/Reset/RESET_TOGRAVE).

Name: RESETS_STANDARD
Summary: Typically used for effects registered to cards they affect.
Description: An [effect](/api/types/Effect) with this [reset value](/api/functions/Effect/SetReset) added to [`RESET_EVENT`](/api/constants/Reset/RESET_EVENT) resets when [the card it's registered to](/api/functions/Effect/GetHandler) [leaves the field](/api/constants/Reset/RESET_LEAVE), [moves to the field](/api/constants/Reset/RESET_TOFIELD), is [flipped face-down](/api/constants/Reset/RESET_TURN_SET), is [banished](/api/constants/Reset/RESET_REMOVE) (even [temporarily](/api/constants/Reset/RESET_TEMP_REMOVE)), or is [sent to the hand](/api/constants/Reset/RESET_TOHAND), [Deck](/api/constants/Reset/RESET_TODECK), or [GY](/api/constants/Reset/RESET_TOGRAVE). This roughly corresponds to when the card stops being face-up in its current location, and is typically used for effects that are registered to the cards they affect.

Name: RESETS_STANDARD_DISABLE
Summary: Typically used for effects that a card registers only to itself.
Description: A composite [reset value](/api/functions/Effect/SetReset) typically used for effects that a card registers only to itself. An [effect](/api/types/Effect) with this reset value added to [`RESET_EVENT`](/api/constants/Reset/RESET_EVENT) resets when [the card it's registered to](/api/functions/Effect/GetHandler) [has its effects negated](/api/constants/Reset/RESET_DISABLE) or [stops being face-up in its current location](/api/constants/Reset/RESETS_STANDARD).

Name: RESETS_STANDARD_DISABLE_PHASE_END
Summary: Typically used for effects that a card registers to itself until the End Phase.
Description: A composite [reset value](/api/functions/Effect/SetReset) typically used for effects that a card registers only to itself until the end of the turn. An [effect](/api/types/Effect) with this reset value resets [in the End Phase](/api/constants/Phase/PHASE_END) or when [the card it's registered to](/api/functions/Effect/GetHandler) [has its effects negated](/api/constants/Reset/RESET_DISABLE) or [stops being face-up in its current location](/api/constants/Reset/RESETS_STANDARD).

Name: RESETS_STANDARD_EXC_GRAVE
Summary: Typically used for effects that negate cards destroyed by battle.
Description: A composite [reset value](/api/functions/Effect/SetReset) typically used for negation effects that negate the effects of cards destroyed by battle (e.g. ["Worm Warlord"](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Worm_Warlord)). An [effect](/api/types/Effect) with this reset value added to [`RESET_EVENT`](/api/constants/Reset/RESET_EVENT) resets when [the card it's registered to](/api/functions/Effect/GetHandler) [stops being face-up in its current location](/api/constants/Reset/RESETS_STANDARD), **except** when it [leaves the field](/api/constants/Reset/RESET_LEAVE) or is [sent to the GY](/api/constants/Reset/RESET_TOGRAVE).

Name: RESETS_STANDARD_PHASE_END
Summary: Typically used for effects registered to cards they affect until the End Phase.
Description: A composite [reset value](/api/functions/Effect/SetReset) typically used for effects that are registered to the cards they affect until the end of the turn. An [effect](/api/types/Effect) with this reset value resets [in the End Phase](/api/constants/Phase/PHASE_END) or when [the card it's registered to](/api/functions/Effect/GetHandler) [stops being face-up in its current location](/api/constants/Reset/RESETS_STANDARD).

Name: RESET_CARD
Summary: Manually resets effects owned by a card with a given passcode.
Description: The reset type used to [manually reset](/api/functions/Card/ResetEffect) [effects](/api/types/Effect) [owned](/api/functions/Effect/GetOwner) by a card with a specified passcode. This cannot be used as a reset value for [`Effect.SetReset`](/api/functions/Effect/SetReset).

Name: RESET_CHAIN
Summary: Resets at the end of Chain resolution.
Description: An [effect](/api/types/Effect) with this [reset value](/api/functions/Effect/SetReset) resets at the end of Chain resolution. This cannot be used as a reset type for [`Card.ResetEffect`](/api/functions/Card/ResetEffect) to manually reset effects.

Name: RESET_CODE
Summary: Manually resets effects with a given effect code.
Description: The reset type used to [manually reset](/api/functions/Card/ResetEffect) [effects](/api/types/Effect) with a specified [effect code](/api/enums/EffectCode). This cannot be used as a reset value for [`Effect.SetReset`](/api/functions/Effect/SetReset).

Name: RESET_CONTROL
Summary: Resets when control of the card changes.
Description: An [effect](/api/types/Effect) with this [reset value](/api/functions/Effect/SetReset) added to [`RESET_EVENT`](/api/constants/Reset/RESET_EVENT) resets when control of [the card it's registered to](/api/functions/Effect/GetHandler) changes.

Name: RESET_COPY
Description: The reset type used to [manually reset](/api/functions/Card/ResetEffect) [effects](/api/types/Effect) that were registered by [copying](/api/functions/Card/CopyEffect), with a specified instance ID. This cannot be used as a reset value for [`Effect.SetReset`](/api/functions/Effect/SetReset).

Name: RESET_DISABLE
Summary: Resets when the effects of the card is negated.
Description: An [effect](/api/types/Effect) with this [reset value](/api/functions/Effect/SetReset) added to [`RESET_EVENT`](/api/constants/Reset/RESET_EVENT) resets when the effects of [the card it's registered to](/api/functions/Effect/GetHandler) is negated.

Name: RESET_EVENT
Summary: Resets when an event happens to the card it's registered to.
Description: An [effect](/api/types/Effect) with this [reset value](/api/functions/Effect/SetReset) resets when an event happens to the card it's registered to. The event(s) is specified by adding [its corresponding reset value](/api/tags/event-reset) to this constant. This can also be used to [manually reset](/api/functions/Card/ResetEffect) effects that reset at the specified event.

Name: RESET_LEAVE
Summary: Resets when the card leaves the field.
Description: An [effect](/api/types/Effect) with this [reset value](/api/functions/Effect/SetReset) added to [`RESET_EVENT`](/api/constants/Reset/RESET_EVENT) resets when [the card it's registered to](/api/functions/Effect/GetHandler) leaves the field.

Name: RESET_MSCHANGE
Summary: Resets when the card moves between the Monster Zone and the Spell/Trap Zone.
Description: An [effect](/api/types/Effect) with this [reset value](/api/functions/Effect/SetReset) added to [`RESET_EVENT`](/api/constants/Reset/RESET_EVENT) resets when [the card it's registered to](/api/functions/Effect/GetHandler) moves from the Monster Zone to the Spell & Trap Zone, or vice versa.

Name: RESET_OPPO_TURN
Summary: Resets only on the opponent's turn.
Description: An [effect](/api/types/Effect) with this [reset value](/api/functions/Effect/SetReset) added to [`RESET_PHASE`](/api/constants/Reset/RESET_PHASE) only resets when the added [phase(s)](/api/enums/Phase) happens on the opponent's turn. If the effect is registered to a card, the opponent is the card's controller's opponent.

Name: RESET_OVERLAY
Summary: Resets when the card becomes attached as an Xyz Material.
Description: An [effect](/api/types/Effect) with this [reset value](/api/functions/Effect/SetReset) added to [`RESET_EVENT`](/api/constants/Reset/RESET_EVENT) resets when [the card it's registered to](/api/functions/Effect/GetHandler) becomes attached as an Xyz Material.

Name: RESET_PHASE
Description: An [effect](/api/types/Effect) with this [reset value](/api/functions/Effect/SetReset) resets in a given phase. The phase(s) is specified by adding [its corresponding phase value](/api/enums/Phase) to this constant. This can also be used to [manually reset](/api/functions/Card/ResetEffect) effects that reset at the specified phase.

Name: RESET_REMOVE
Summary: Resets when the card is banished.
Description: An [effect](/api/types/Effect) with this [reset value](/api/functions/Effect/SetReset) added to [`RESET_EVENT`](/api/constants/Reset/RESET_EVENT) resets when [the card it's registered to](/api/functions/Effect/GetHandler) is banished.

Name: RESET_SELF_TURN
Summary: Resets only on the player's own turn.
Description: An [effect](/api/types/Effect) with this [reset value](/api/functions/Effect/SetReset) added to [`RESET_PHASE`](/api/constants/Reset/RESET_PHASE) only resets when the added [phase(s)](/api/enums/Phase) happens on the player's own turn. If the effect is registered to a card, the player is the card's controller.

Name: RESET_TEMP_REMOVE
Summary: Resets when the card is temporarily banished.
Description: An [effect](/api/types/Effect) with this [reset value](/api/functions/Effect/SetReset) added to [`RESET_EVENT`](/api/constants/Reset/RESET_EVENT) resets when [the card it's registered to](/api/functions/Effect/GetHandler) is temporarily banished.

Name: RESET_TODECK
Summary: Resets when the card is sent to the Deck.
Description: An [effect](/api/types/Effect) with this [reset value](/api/functions/Effect/SetReset) added to [`RESET_EVENT`](/api/constants/Reset/RESET_EVENT) resets when [the card it's registered to](/api/functions/Effect/GetHandler) is sent to the Deck.

Name: RESET_TOFIELD
Summary: Resets when the card moves to the field.
Description: An [effect](/api/types/Effect) with this [reset value](/api/functions/Effect/SetReset) added to [`RESET_EVENT`](/api/constants/Reset/RESET_EVENT) resets when [the card it's registered to](/api/functions/Effect/GetHandler) moves to the field. Returning to the field, such as after being temporarily banished, is not included.

Name: RESET_TOGRAVE
Summary: Resets when the card is sent to the GY.
Description: An [effect](/api/types/Effect) with this [reset value](/api/functions/Effect/SetReset) added to [`RESET_EVENT`](/api/constants/Reset/RESET_EVENT) resets when [the card it's registered to](/api/functions/Effect/GetHandler) is sent to the GY.

Name: RESET_TOHAND
Summary: Resets when the card is sent to the hand.
Description: An [effect](/api/types/Effect) with this [reset value](/api/functions/Effect/SetReset) added to [`RESET_EVENT`](/api/constants/Reset/RESET_EVENT) resets when [the card it's registered to](/api/functions/Effect/GetHandler) is sent to the hand.

Name: RESET_TURN_SET
Summary: Resets when the card is flipped face-down.
Description: An [effect](/api/types/Effect) with this [reset value](/api/functions/Effect/SetReset) added to [`RESET_EVENT`](/api/constants/Reset/RESET_EVENT) resets when [the card it's registered to](/api/functions/Effect/GetHandler) is flipped face-down.

Name: RITPROC_EQUAL
Summary: Uses Ritual Materials whose total Levels exactly equal the Ritual Monster's.
Description: A Ritual Summoning [effect](/api/types/Effect] created with this Level type uses Ritual Materials whose total Levels exactly equal the Ritual Monster's Level.

Name: RITPROC_GREATER
Summary: Uses Ritual Materials whose total Levels equal or exceed the Ritual Monster's.
Description: A Ritual Summoning [effect](/api/types/Effect] created with this Level type uses Ritual Materials whose total Levels equal or exceed the Ritual Monster's Level.

Name: HINT_SKILL
Description: Sets the displayed Skill Card, specified by its passcode. If this is set first before [`HINT_SKILL_COVER`](/api/constants/SkillHint/HINT_SKILL_COVER), the Skill is displayed face-up.

Name: HINT_SKILL_COVER
Summary: Sets the cover of the displayed Skill Card.
Description: Sets the cover of the displayed Skill Card, specified by its passcode as the hint value. If this is set first before [`HINT_SKILL`](/api/constants/SkillHint/HINT_SKILL), the Skill is displayed face-down. The Skill Card can also be set by adding `code<<32` to the hint value, where `code` is its passcode.

Name: HINT_SKILL_FLIP
Summary: Sets the position of the displayed Skill Card.
Description: Sets the position of the displayed Skill Card (face-up or face-down). The hint value should be `0x100000000` for face-up and `0x200000000` for face-down. The displayed Skill Card can also be changed by adding the new Skill Card's passcode to the hint value.

Name: HINT_SKILL_REMOVE
Description: Removes the displayed Skill Card.

Name: MATERIAL_FUSION
Description: The type for Summons that use [Fusion Materials](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Fusion_Material).

Name: MATERIAL_LINK
Description: The type for Summons that use [Link Materials](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Link_Material).

Name: MATERIAL_SYNCHRO
Description: The type for Summons that use [Synchro Materials](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Synchro_Material).

Name: MATERIAL_XYZ
Description: The type for Summons that use [Xyz Materials](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Xyz_Material).

Name: SUMMON_BY_NOUVELLES
Summary: Special Summon by the effect of a "Nouvelles" monster.
Description: The type for Special Summons performed by the effect of a ["Nouvelles"](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Nouvelles) monster.

Name: SUMMON_TYPE_FLIP
Description: The type for [Flip Summons](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Flip_Summon).

Name: SUMMON_TYPE_FUSION
Description: The type for [Fusion Summons](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Fusion_Summon).

Name: SUMMON_TYPE_GEMINI
Summary: Normal Summon of a Gemini monster on the field.
Description: The type for the [Normal Summon of Gemini monsters while they are already on the field](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Normal_Summon#Normal_Summoning_Gemini_monsters_again).

Name: SUMMON_TYPE_LINK
Description: The type for [Link Summons](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Link_Summon).

Name: SUMMON_TYPE_MAXIMUM
Description: The type for [Maximum Summons](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Maximum_Summon).

Name: SUMMON_TYPE_NORMAL
Description: The type for [Normal Summons](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Normal_Summon).

Name: SUMMON_TYPE_PENDULUM
Description: The type for [Pendulum Summons](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Pendulum_Summon).

Name: SUMMON_TYPE_RITUAL
Description: The type for [Ritual Summons](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Ritual_Summon).

Name: SUMMON_TYPE_SPECIAL
Description: The type for [Special Summons](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Special_Summon).

Name: SUMMON_TYPE_SYNCHRO
Description: The type for [Synchro Summons](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Synchro_Summon).

Name: SUMMON_TYPE_TRIBUTE
Description: The type for [Tribute Summons](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Tribute_Summon).

Name: SUMMON_TYPE_XYZ
Description: The type for [Xyz Summons](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Xyz_Summon).

Name: SUMMON_WITH_MONSTER_REBORN
Summary: Special Summon by the effect of a "Monster Reborn".
Description: The type for Special Summons performed by the effect of a ["Monster Reborn"](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Monster_Reborn).

Name: TIMINGS_CHECK_MONSTER
Summary: Prompts when a monster(s) is Normal, Flip, or Special Summoned.
Description: An effect with this [hint timing](/api/functions/Effect/SetHintTiming) will be prompted for activation when a monster(s) is [Normal](/api/constants/Timing/TIMING_SUMMON), [Flip](/api/constants/Timing/TIMING_FLIPSUMMON), or [Special Summoned](/api/constants/Timing/TIMING_SPSUMMON).

Name: TIMINGS_CHECK_MONSTER_E
Summary: Prompts when a monster(s) is Summoned and in the End Phase.
Description: An effect with this [hint timing](/api/functions/Effect/SetHintTiming) will be prompted for activation [when a monster(s) is Normal, Flip, or Special Summoned](/api/constants/Timing/TIMINGS_CHECK_MONSTER) and [in the End Phase](/api/constants/Timing/TIMING_END_PHASE).

Name: TIMING_ATTACK
Summary: Prompts when an attack is declared.
Description: An effect with this [hint timing](/api/functions/Effect/SetHintTiming) will be prompted for activation when an attack is declared.

Name: TIMING_BATTLED
Description: An effect with this [hint timing](/api/functions/Effect/SetHintTiming) will be prompted for activation after [damage calculation](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Damage_calculation).

Name: TIMING_BATTLE_END
Summary: Prompts when the Battle Phase is about to end.
Description: An effect with this [hint timing](/api/functions/Effect/SetHintTiming) will be prompted for activation when the [Battle Phase](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Battle_Phase) is about to end, before entering the [Main Phase 2](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Main_Phase_2) or [End Phase](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/End_Phase).

Name: TIMING_BATTLE_PHASE
Summary: Prompts after attack declaration and before entering the Damage Step.
Description: An effect with this [hint timing](/api/functions/Effect/SetHintTiming) will be prompted for activation during the [Battle Step](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Battle_Step) after attack declaration and before entering the [Damage Step](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Damage_Step).

Name: TIMING_BATTLE_START
Summary: Prompts at the start of the Battle Phase.
Description: An effect with this [hint timing](/api/functions/Effect/SetHintTiming) will be prompted for activation at the start of the [Battle Phase](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Battle_Phase).

Name: TIMING_BATTLE_STEP_END
Summary: Prompts when the player is attempting to end the Battle Phase.
Description: An effect with this [hint timing](/api/functions/Effect/SetHintTiming) will be prompted for activation when the player is attempting to end the [Battle Phase](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Battle_Phase).

Name: TIMING_CHAIN_END
Summary: Prompts at the end of Chain resolution.
Description: An effect with this [hint timing](/api/functions/Effect/SetHintTiming) will be prompted for activation at the end of Chain resolution.

Name: TIMING_DAMAGE
Summary: Prompts when a player takes damage.
Description: An effect with this [hint timing](/api/functions/Effect/SetHintTiming) will be prompted for activation when a player takes damage.

Name: TIMING_DAMAGE_CAL
Summary: Prompts at the start of damage calculation.
Description: An effect with this [hint timing](/api/functions/Effect/SetHintTiming) will be prompted for activation at the start of [damage calculation](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Damage_calculation).

Name: TIMING_DAMAGE_STEP
Summary: Prompts at the start of the Damage Step.
Description: An effect with this [hint timing](/api/functions/Effect/SetHintTiming) will be prompted for activation at the start of the [Damage Step](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Damage_Step), before [damage calculation](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Damage_calculation).

Name: TIMING_DESTROY
Summary: Prompts when a card(s) is destroyed.
Description: An effect with this [hint timing](/api/functions/Effect/SetHintTiming) will be prompted for activation when a card(s) is destroyed.

Name: TIMING_DRAW
Summary: Prompts when a card(s) is drawn.
Description: An effect with this [hint timing](/api/functions/Effect/SetHintTiming) will be prompted for activation when a card(s) is drawn.

Name: TIMING_DRAW_PHASE
Description: An effect with this [hint timing](/api/functions/Effect/SetHintTiming) will be prompted for activation in the [Draw Phase](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Draw_Phase), after the player has performed their normal draw.

Name: TIMING_END_PHASE
Description: An effect with this [hint timing](/api/functions/Effect/SetHintTiming) will be prompted for activation in the [End Phase](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/End_Phase).

Name: TIMING_EQUIP
Summary: Prompts when a card is equipped to another.
Description: An effect with this [hint timing](/api/functions/Effect/SetHintTiming) will be prompted for activation when a card is equipped to another.

Name: TIMING_FLIPSUMMON
Summary: Prompts when a monster is Flip Summoned.
Description: An effect with this [hint timing](/api/functions/Effect/SetHintTiming) will be prompted for activation when a monster is [Flip Summoned](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Flip_Summon).

Name: TIMING_MAIN_END
Summary: Prompts when the Main Phase is about to end.
Description: An effect with this [hint timing](/api/functions/Effect/SetHintTiming) will be prompted for activation when the [Main Phase](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Main_Phase) is about to end, before entering the [Battle Phase](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Battle_Phase) or [End Phase](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/End_Phase).

Name: TIMING_MSET
Summary: Prompts when a monster is Normal Set.
Description: An effect with this [hint timing](/api/functions/Effect/SetHintTiming) will be prompted for activation when a monster is [Normal Set](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Normal_Set).

Name: TIMING_POS_CHANGE
Summary: Prompts when a monster's battle position is changed.
Description: An effect with this [hint timing](/api/functions/Effect/SetHintTiming) will be prompted for activation when a monster's battle position is changed.

Name: TIMING_RECOVER
Summary: Prompts when a player gains LP.
Description: An effect with this [hint timing](/api/functions/Effect/SetHintTiming) will be prompted for activation when a player gains LP.

Name: TIMING_REMOVE
Summary: Prompts when a card(s) is banished.
Description: An effect with this [hint timing](/api/functions/Effect/SetHintTiming) will be prompted for activation when a card(s) is banished.

Name: TIMING_SPSUMMON
Summary: Prompts when a monster is Special Summoned.
Description: An effect with this [hint timing](/api/functions/Effect/SetHintTiming) will be prompted for activation when a monster is [Special Summoned](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Special_Summon).

Name: TIMING_SSET
Summary: Prompts when a Spell/Trap Card is Set.
Description: An effect with this [hint timing](/api/functions/Effect/SetHintTiming) will be prompted for activation when a Spell/Trap Card is [Set](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Set).

Name: TIMING_STANDBY_PHASE
Description: An effect with this [hint timing](/api/functions/Effect/SetHintTiming) will be prompted for activation in the [Standby Phase](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Standby_Phase).

Name: TIMING_SUMMON
Summary: Prompts when a monster is Normal Summoned.
Description: An effect with this [hint timing](/api/functions/Effect/SetHintTiming) will be prompted for activation when a monster is [Normal Summoned](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Normal_Summon).

Name: TIMING_TODECK
Summary: Prompts when a card(s) is sent to the Deck.
Description: An effect with this [hint timing](/api/functions/Effect/SetHintTiming) will be prompted for activation when a card(s) is sent to the Deck.

Name: TIMING_TOGRAVE
Summary: Prompts when a card(s) is sent to the GY.
Description: An effect with this [hint timing](/api/functions/Effect/SetHintTiming) will be prompted for activation when a card(s) is sent to the GY.

Name: TIMING_TOHAND
Summary: Prompts when a card(s) is sent to the hand.
Description: An effect with this [hint timing](/api/functions/Effect/SetHintTiming) will be prompted for activation when a card(s) is sent to the hand.

Description: The reason value for a Duel win caused by ["Holactie the Creator of Light"](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Holactie_the_Creator_of_Light).

Description: The reason value for a Duel win caused by ["Creator of Miracles"](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Creator_of_Miracles).

Name: WIN_REASON_DECK_MASTER

Description: The reason value for a Duel win caused by ["Destiny Board"](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Destiny_Board).

Description: The reason value for a Duel win caused by ["Number C88: Gimmick Puppet Disaster Leo"](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Number_C88:_Gimmick_Puppet_Disaster_Leo).

Name: WIN_REASON_DRAW_OF_FATE
Summary: Victory by the effect of "Draw of Fate"
Description: The reason value for a Duel win caused by ["Draw of Fate"](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Draw_of_Fate).

Name: WIN_REASON_EVIL_1
Description: This reason value is unused, since ["Evil 1"](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Evil_1) does not actually have an  alternate win condition.

Status.message: Value is unused. "Evil 1" does not have an alternate win condition.

Name: WIN_REASON_EXODIA

Description: The reason value for a Duel win caused by ["Exodia, the Forbidden One"](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Exodia, the_Forbidden_One).

Summary: Victory by the effect of "Exodia, the Legendary Defender"
Description: The reason value for a Duel win caused by ["Exodia, the Legendary Defender"](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Exodia,_the_Legendary_Defender).

Summary: Victory by the effect of "Exodius the Ultimate Forbidden Lord"
Description: The reason value for a Duel win caused by ["Exodius the Ultimate Forbidden Lord"](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Exodius_the_Ultimate_Forbidden_Lord).

Name: WIN_REASON_FA_WINNERS
Summary: Victory by the effect of "F.A. Winners"
Description: The reason value for a Duel win caused by ["F.A. Winners"](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/F.A._Winners).

Name: WIN_REASON_FINAL_COUNTDOWN
Summary: Victory by the effect of "Final Countdown"
Description: The reason value for a Duel win caused by ["Final Countdown"](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Final_Countdown).

Name: WIN_REASON_FINAL_DRAW
Summary: Victory by the effect of "Final Draw"
Description: The reason value for a Duel win caused by ["Final Draw"](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Final_Draw).

Name: WIN_REASON_FLYING_ELEPHANT
Summary: Victory by the effect of "Flying Elephant"
Description: The reason value for a Duel win caused by ["Flying Elephant"](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Flying_Elephant).

Name: WIN_REASON_GHOSTRICK_MISCHIEF
Summary: Victory by the effect of "Ghostrick Angel of Mischief"
Description: The reason value for a Duel win caused by ["Ghostrick Angel of Mischief"](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Ghostrick_Angel_of_Mischief).

Name: WIN_REASON_JACKPOT7
Description: The reason value for a Duel win caused by ["Jackpot 7"](https://yugipedia.com/wiki/Jackpot_7).

Name: WIN_REASON_LAST_TURN
